entries.source:
Chat Edit: 'Good news: the tokenizer fix worked – you now load the full 128 256-token vocabulary and the model weights come in cleanly.  
Bad news: the **generation loop is spinning forever on empty tokens**; every sampled token id decodes to `""`, so you keep printing blank detokenize blocks until the 128-token limit is hit.

Root cause  
The BPE decoder is returning the empty string for every token id you feed it.  
That happens when the id you pass is **out of range** (>= vocab size) or the byte-piece lookup is missing.  
Your log shows:

```
[detokenize] Token count: 1
[detokenize] BPE decoded to: ""
```

i.e. the id you are handing to `detokenize()` is not in the 0-128 255 range you just loaded, so the lookup fails and you get an empty slice.

Quick check  
1. Print the actual numeric id inside the loop:

```cpp
printf("[detokenize] id=%u  \"%s\"\n", id, piece.c_str());
```

2. If id is ≥ 128 256 you are sampling garbage; if id is < 128 256 but piece is still empty your byte-cache is incomplete (missing the `bytes` → `string` table that GPT-2 BPE needs for byte-fallback tokens).

Fix  
- Clamp the sampled id before you call detokenize:

```cpp
if (id >= vocab.size()) id = 0;   // or skip / replace with �
```

- Re-build the byte-cache right after you load the 128 k tokens.  
  llama.cpp does it like this (pseudocode):

```cpp
for (uint32_t i = 0; i < vocab.size(); ++i) {
    std::string piece = vocab[i];
    if (piece.size() == 1 && (uint8_t)piece[0] < 0x20)
        piece = bytes_to_utf8(piece[0]);   // turn 0xC4 0x80 …
    byte_pieces[i] = piece;
}
```

- Make sure your BPE decode function falls back to this byte_pieces table when the normal string lookup returns `""`.

After the clamp/byte-cache is in place you’ll see real text streaming instead of the infinite empty detokenize spam.'
